briefs.title: Council to consider Land Acknowledgement at Delta meeting
briefs.summary: Delta Council will consider a Land Acknowledgement at its upcoming meeting on April 27, 2026. This is a formal recognition of the Indigenous peoples on whose traditional territory the meeting takes place. No regulatory changes or business obligations are associated with this agenda item.

briefs.title: Council to consider: Delta approves its April 27, 2026 meeting agenda
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on endorsing the official agenda for its April 27, 2026 Regular Council meeting. This is a routine procedural step — it simply confirms which items will be discussed and decided at that meeting. No business-specific rules or requirements are being proposed at this time.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: No Details Available for Delta Presentations Agenda Item
briefs.summary: The agenda lists a 'Presentations' item for the Delta Council meeting on April 27, 2026, but no supporting content was provided. It is not possible to summarize what Council will consider or how it may affect your business without the underlying details.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Delta Council agenda at delta.ca for presentation details before the April 27, 2026 meeting.

key_dates.label: Delta Council meeting — Presentations agenda item

briefs.title: Council to Receive: Delta Earth Day Photo Contest 2026 Winners Presentation
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to receive an informational presentation on the winners of the 2026 Earth Day Photo Contest. This is not a decision item — no vote or bylaw is involved. The presentation simply recognizes winners across child, youth, and adult categories, including a grand prize winner.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Delta's Spring–Summer 2026 Seasonal Hazard Preparedness Briefing
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to receive a presentation on seasonal hazards and emergency preparedness for Spring–Summer 2026. The briefing, led by Emergency Program Manager Jeff Owens, will cover forecasted risks and how the City plans to respond — giving Council an overview of what businesses and residents may face in the coming months.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's response — hazard forecasts may affect your business continuity planning this spring and summer.

key_dates.label: Delta Council seasonal hazard preparedness presentation — Spring–Summer 2026

briefs.title: Council to observe Day of Mourning for workers killed or injured on the job
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to receive a report and observe a one-minute moment of silence at its April 27, 2026 meeting to mark the National Day of Mourning on April 28 — a day that honours workers who have been killed or injured on the job. As part of the observance, all flags at Municipal Hall will be flown at half-mast on April 28, and a public ceremony is planned at Rotary Park at 10:45 a.m.
briefs.action_required: Consider attending the April 28 ceremony at Rotary Park at 10:45 a.m. to honour workers.

key_dates.label: Day of Mourning ceremony at Rotary Park, Delta Municipal Hall

briefs.title: Council to consider receiving closed meeting items into the public record
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on formally receiving into the public record the items that arose from its April 13, 2026 closed (in-camera) meeting — meaning those items would be officially acknowledged in the open meeting minutes. This is a routine procedural step and does not appear to introduce any new rules or requirements for businesses.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Delta Consent Agenda Items 7.1–7.5 Up for Approval
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on adopting five consent agenda items (7.1 to 7.5) in a single grouped vote at its April 27, 2026 meeting. A consent agenda is a package of routine items approved all at once — no details about what those five items cover are provided in this notice.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Delta Council agenda for items 7.1–7.5 to see if any affect your business.

key_dates.label: Delta Council consent agenda items 7.1–7.5 vote date

briefs.title: Council to vote on approving minutes from Delta's April 13 closed-door meeting
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on formally adopting the minutes from its April 13, 2026 Special Council meeting, which was held 'in-camera' (meaning closed to the public). That closed session covered topics including a law enforcement matter, early-stage talks about a possible new municipal service, and a labour or employee relations issue.

briefs.title: Council to vote on adopting April 13, 2026 Delta Regular Council Meeting Minutes
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on formally adopting the minutes from its April 13, 2026 Regular Council meeting. Those minutes cover several decisions, including new highway bylaws, a rezoning application for a nine-unit townhouse, a primary care needs assessment, and support for the Royal Canadian Legion's 100th anniversary celebration.
briefs.action_required: Review the April 13 meeting minutes if any decisions affect your business or property.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Delta Council/School Board Liaison Meeting Minutes from March 31, 2026
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on receiving the minutes from the March 31, 2026, Delta Council / Delta Board of Education Liaison Committee meeting into the official record. The meeting covered updates on several local infrastructure projects — including track and field upgrades at Seaquam Secondary, North Delta, and South Delta Secondary schools, the Winskill Renewal Project, and the Youth Hub program operating out of the North Delta Recreation Centre.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Delta Small-Scale Multi-Unit Housing Phase 2 Update and Next Steps
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to receive a community engagement summary on proposed zoning changes that would support small-scale multi-unit housing (SSMUH) — meaning more housing types like duplexes, garden suites, and houseplexes on residential lots. Staff will continue monitoring current rules and plan to develop a new standalone houseplex zone for future Council consideration, with a key deadline of May 30, 2026 tied to federal Housing Accelerator Fund requirements.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — new houseplex zoning rules could affect your development or property plans.

key_dates.label: Delta HAF Action Plan SSMUH amendments completion deadline
Delta SSMUH Phase 2 staff report-back to Council target year

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Delta Farmers Institute Letter on Canoe Pass Marine Operations
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to formally receive a letter from the Delta Farmers Institute, dated April 13, 2026, asking for a review of marine operations in Canoe Pass Inlet. The letter was prompted by the temporary closure of the Westham Island swing bridge for emergency repairs, which has disrupted boat traffic in the area and affected local farmers and other users of the waterway.
briefs.action_required: If your business uses Canoe Pass Inlet, monitor Delta Council updates on the marine operations review.

briefs.title: Council to vote: Delta bylaws up for final adoption — April 27, 2026
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on one or more bylaws for final adoption at its April 27, 2026 meeting. No details about the specific bylaws were included in the agenda item provided, so the exact rules or changes up for approval are not yet known. Business owners in Delta should check the full agenda on the City of Delta's website to see if any proposed rules could affect their operations.
briefs.action_required: Check Delta's full council agenda at delta.ca to find out which bylaws are up for final adoption.

key_dates.label: Delta Council final bylaw adoption vote — meeting date

briefs.title: Council to vote on rezoning Delta property at 7660 116A Street for residential subdivision
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on final adoption of Bylaw No. 8503, which would rezone a property at 7660 116A Street to allow it to be split into two smaller single-family lots. If approved, the zoning would change from a duplex/single-detached category (RD3) to a single-detached residential category (RS7), with each new lot measuring about 37 ft wide and 120 ft deep.
briefs.action_required: If this rezoning affects your nearby property or business, attend the April 27, 2026 Delta Council meeting.

key_dates.label: Delta Bylaw No. 8503 rezoning — final adoption vote at Council meeting

briefs.title: Council to Consider New Bylaws at Delta's April 27, 2026 Meeting
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on giving first, second, and third reading to a set of new bylaws at its April 27, 2026 meeting. The specific content of these bylaws has not been detailed in the agenda item, but business owners in Delta should watch for updates, as new bylaws can affect local rules, permits, or operations.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Delta Council agenda for bylaw details before the April 27, 2026 meeting.

key_dates.label: Delta Council bylaw readings — first, second, and third reading vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to vote on Delta's 2026 property tax rates, including Tsawwassen BIA levy
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on three bylaws that would set property tax rates for 2026. If approved, the bylaws would lock in a 2.9% municipal tax increase — about $90 more per year for a typical home — plus collect taxes on behalf of Metro Vancouver and charge a special levy on 134 properties in the Tsawwassen Business Improvement Area.
briefs.action_required: If you own property in the Tsawwassen BIA, note your 2026 tax bill will include a BIA levy of $0.3311 per $1,000 of assessed value.

key_dates.label: Delta 2026 property tax payment due date
Delta 2026 property tax — first 5% late penalty applies
Delta 2026 property tax — second 5% late penalty applies

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Delta Council to vote on 16-unit houseplex development in Tsawwassen
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on a proposed zoning change that would allow two vacant lots at 809 51 Street and 5140 8A Avenue in Tsawwassen to be split into four smaller lots, with a four-unit houseplex (a building containing four attached homes) built on each — creating 16 new homes in total. Before the bylaw can be fully adopted, the property owner must sign a legal covenant (a binding promise registered on the land title) covering building design and landscaping, and grant an easement (a legal right) allowing shared driveway access between the lots.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Delta Council decision — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and neighbourhood density.

key_dates.label: Delta Council meeting — vote on Bylaw No. 8499 zoning amendment readings
Delta Housing Target Order — occupancy deadline for units to count toward provincial target

briefs.title: Proposed: Delta Council to vote on rezoning for 16-unit townhouse development on 60B Street
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on a proposed zoning change that would allow a developer to build 16 four-bedroom townhouse units across two three-storey buildings at 4739 and 4749 60B Street in East Ladner. If approved, the owner must meet several conditions first — including submitting final building and landscape plans, paying a $16,000 community amenity contribution (a fee that funds local parks and recreation), and getting sign-off from the provincial Ministry of Transportation and Transit.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on April 27, 2026 — this rezoning could affect nearby property values and neighbourhood density.

key_dates.label: Delta Council meeting — rezoning bylaw No. 8565 vote date

briefs.action_required: Check the full Delta Council agenda for details on the correspondence before the April 27 meeting.

No details about the specific items were provided in the agenda excerpt, so business owners should check the full agenda on the City of Delta website to see if any proposals could affect their operations.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Delta Council agenda for April 27, 2026 at delta.ca for details that may affect your business.

key_dates.label: Delta Council meeting — Administrative Reports reviewed

briefs.title: Council to Consider Endorsing Delta's 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Award Applications
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on endorsing two award applications to the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) — a provincial organization that recognizes standout local government work. The two submissions are the Westham Island Bridge emergency response (nominated for Excellence in Governance) and the new 16 km Barns to Beaches Bike Route connecting Ladner to Tsawwassen (nominated for Excellence in Service Delivery).
briefs.action_required: If your business was affected by the Westham Island Bridge closure or benefits from the Barns to Beaches route, watch for the council vote.

key_dates.label: UBCM Community Excellence Awards application window closes

briefs.title: Council to Consider Traffic Safety Upgrades Near Holly Elementary in Ladner
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on a package of traffic safety improvements near Holly Elementary School at the intersection of Ladner Trunk Road and 62 Street. The proposed changes include speed reader boards, longer pedestrian crossing times, a longer left-turn lane, and a relocated school zone sign — all aimed at making the area safer for kids walking to and from school.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near Holly Elementary or Ladner Trunk Road, watch for construction activity starting after the April 27 vote.

key_dates.label: Delta Council vote — Ladner Trunk Road and 62 Street safety enhancements

briefs.title: Council to vote on appointing new Approving Officer for Delta subdivision applications
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on appointing Anne Berry, General Manager of Development, as an Approving Officer — a role that gives someone the authority to review, approve, or deny applications to subdivide land. Council will also consider revoking the previous appointment held by Doreann Mayhew. If approved, Delta would have four Approving Officers in total.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you have a subdivision application pending with the City of Delta.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Heritage Permit for New Duplex at 4488 Arthur Drive, Delta
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on a heritage alteration permit — a special approval required before changing or building in a designated heritage area — that would allow the demolition of an existing house and construction of a new two-unit duplex with secondary suites at 4488 Arthur Drive in Ladner. The property sits within the Ladner Heritage Conservation Area, so Council approval is needed before any work can begin.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision if you own or develop property in the Ladner Heritage Conservation Area.

key_dates.label: Delta Council meeting — Heritage Alteration Permit 4488 Arthur Drive vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Delta's Response to Maple Ridge Industrial Land Expansion Proposal
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on whether to tell Metro Vancouver that Delta has no objection to a proposed regional land-use change in Maple Ridge. The proposal would redesignate (change the official land-use category of) roughly 301 hectares near 256 Street and 128 Avenue from Rural and Industrial to Industrial and Conservation & Recreation, creating a light industrial hub and expanding the Urban Containment Boundary — the line that separates urban development areas from rural land.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near the Maple Ridge 256 Street area, monitor this proposal as it moves through Metro Vancouver approval.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Delta's Response to Surrey Hazelmere Land Use Amendment Proposal
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on whether to submit a 'no comment' response to Metro Vancouver regarding a proposed amendment to Metro 2050 — the regional plan that guides land use and growth across Metro Vancouver. The City of Surrey is asking to redesignate (change the official land-use category of) a 23.7-hectare area in Hazelmere from Rural to General Urban, which would allow a 145-lot subdivision and expand the Urban Containment Boundary to include the site.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Delta Council decision — this regional land use change could affect development and agriculture near Surrey's Hazelmere area.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Agriculture, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider Supporting Delta Gymnastics Society's 'Seniors Can Move' Funding Applications
briefs.summary: Delta Council will consider a motion asking the Mayor to write a letter of support for the Delta Gymnastics Society's 'Seniors Can Move' program. This letter would back the organization's applications for outside funding — it does not commit any city money, but signals official municipal support for the program.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Meeting with Minister to Discuss Healthcare Services in Delta
briefs.summary: Delta Council is set to vote on arranging a meeting with BC Infrastructure Minister Ma to discuss improving healthcare services in Delta, including the proposed Beedie Long-Term Care Centre. This follows a letter from the Ministry dated April 22, 2026, in which the Minister offered to meet with Council directly.
briefs.action_required: If you work in healthcare in Delta, watch for updates from this meeting — it could shape local care services.
